Wired deps: f6i1 depends on dfwx; xk0z.21 depends on both (closes when both close); xk0z.23 now depends on f6i1.

The "Depends on" line in this bead's description (which referenced "xk0z.21 implementation" as text) is now backed by a real bd edge. xk0z.23 will surface in `bd ready` once f6i1 closes.

title: Corrigendum: Rust/WASM frontend re-discovery (forge-qtzs was wrong)
description:
## Context

forge-qtzs (closed 2026-04-28) reported 'HONEST GAP — zero Claude skills for Dioxus / rust-wasm / leptos / yew / wasm-bindgen / trunk across 8 author marketplaces + 7 GitHub topic pages.' That finding is WRONG. User surfaced 12 sources containing 17+ relevant candidates the sub-agent missed, including direct Dioxus skills (Nevaberry@dioxus-knowledge-patch covers Dioxus 0.7.4 specifically — current!) and adjacent Rust+WASM bundles.

This bead re-runs the discovery with a corrected methodology (Rule 11 candidate) and produces a corrigendum to rust-wasm-frontend.md.

## Why the original missed it

Sub-agent searched: anthropics/skills, vercel-labs, openai/skills, microsoft, dioxuslabs, wshobson, Jeffallan, alirezarezvani, plus VoltAgent + travisvn awesome-lists, plus 7 GitHub topic pages (claude-skills, claude-code, agent-skills, dioxus, leptos, yew, wasm-bindgen, trunk).

Did NOT search:
- Personal dotfiles repos with `.claude/skills/` directories (Daiki48/dotfiles)
- Idiosyncratic-naming personal-skills repos (sirius-cc-wu, InSelfControll, Nevaberry)
- Tauri-adjacent repos (dchuk/claude-code-tauri-skills) for transferable Rust-frontend patterns
- Plugin-marketplace organization repos (pluginagentmarketplace/custom-plugin-rust)
- Polymath-style multi-domain repos with non-standard SKILL.md naming (rand/cc-polymath uses INDEX.md + topic-specific .md files, NOT SKILL.md)
- Domain-specific Rust+WASM ecosystems (multiversx/mx-ai-skills — blockchain Rust+WASM smart contracts)
- Adjacent: low-level-dev systems repos (mohitmishra786/low-level-dev-skills)

## Candidates to verify (17+ surfaced by user)

### Direct Dioxus / Rust-frontend (9)
1. Nevaberry/nevaberry-plugins — `dioxus-knowledge-patch` — MIT, 246 LOC, Dioxus 0.7.4 specific (Signals/RSX/server-fns/asset!()/dx CLI). **TOP PICK CANDIDATE.**
2. Daiki48/dotfiles `.claude/skills/dioxus-guide` — 213 LOC, Dioxus 0.7.x desktop+web; signals/rsx!/hooks/Context/async. License unspecified.
3. Daiki48/dotfiles `.claude/skills/leptos-guide` — Leptos sibling framework.
4. Daiki48/dotfiles `.claude/skills/rust-fullstack` — Rust full-stack.
5. sirius-cc-wu/sirius-skills — `dioxus-ui-ux` — 151 LOC, UI patterns: 50 styles + 21 palettes + 50 font pairings; daisyui+shadcn+html-tailwind stacks.
6. sirius-cc-wu/sirius-skills — `dioxus-stitch` — 34 LOC, Stitch design exports → Dioxus + daisyUI.
7. InSelfControll/ai-agent-skills — `rust_dioxus_framework` — 57 LOC, Dioxus cross-platform (web/desktop/mobile); signals/SSR/Tailwind/cargo-manganis. Response tag `[DIOXUS-DEV]`.
8. dchuk/claude-code-tauri-skills — `tauri-frontend-rust` — 431 LOC, Tauri v2 + Leptos primary; Yew/Dioxus/Sycamore secondary; Trunk bundler. **Tauri-specific (Forge isn't Tauri) but multi-framework Rust frontend patterns transfer.**
9. dchuk/claude-code-tauri-skills — `tauri-csp` — 422 LOC, Tauri v2 CSP — framework-specific (Leptos/Yew/Dioxus); browser-CSP transferable.

### Adjacent Rust+WASM bundles (8+)
10. pluginagentmarketplace/custom-plugin-rust — 12-skill bundle: async-programming, cargo-ecosystem, error-handling, ownership-borrowing, rust-cli, rust-concurrency, rust-docker, rust-macros, rust-performance, rust-testing, **rust-wasm**, trait-generics. The rust-wasm skill is the direct hit.
11. mohitmishra786/low-level-dev-skills — 11-skill bundle: binaries, build-systems, compilers, debuggers, embedded, low-level-programming, observability, profilers, runtimes, **rust**, zig. Adjacent more than direct.
12. rand/cc-polymath/skills/wasm — INDEX.md + wasm-browser-integration.md + wasm-fundamentals.md + wasm-rust-toolchain.md + wasm-server-side.md. **NON-STANDARD STRUCTURE: no SKILL.md, uses topic-specific .md files.**
13. rand/cc-polymath/skills/discover-wasm — SKILL.md exists. WASM-discovery slug.
14. rand/cc-polymath/skills/rust — 25 PyO3-focused modules. `pyo3-async-embedded-wasm` and `pyo3-web-frameworks` adjacent.
15. multiversx/mx-ai-skills — 25 MultiversX-specific skills. `multiversx-wasm-debug`, `multiversx-smart-contracts`, `multiversx-dapp-frontend` are Rust+WASM (blockchain context, but transferable patterns for browser WASM debugging).

### Non-skill REFERENCE find (1)
16. **alphaonedev/openclaw-graph** — NOT A SKILL. Neo4j-based graph backend storing 316 OpenClaw skills across 27 clusters. Replaces flat markdown files with Cypher queries. 28.6% Rust codebase (neo4j-sync daemon — event-driven FSEvents file watcher, ~3.8 MB binary, <500ms write-back latency). **DIRECTLY COMPARABLE TO FORGE'S SkillGraphRuntime work (forge-ucc2 + forge-afyg shipped petgraph + linear-scan HnswIndex seam).** Cross-architecture comparison candidate — should be cataloged in clusters.md or a separate architecture-survey doc, NOT installed as a skill.

## Domains in scope (corrected)
- dioxus (PRIMARY — multiple verified candidates exist)
- leptos (sibling — at least one verified candidate)
- yew (covered in tauri-frontend-rust + tauri-csp)
- wasm-bindgen (likely covered in rust-wasm + cc-polymath/wasm)
- trunk (covered in tauri-frontend-rust)
- rust-fullstack (general)
- WASM (browser + server-side)

## Methodology — apply 8 stable + Rule 11 candidate

Per the prior phase memories (project_skill_comparison_phase_3_shipped_2026_04_28 + project_skill_discovery_phase_4_8_shipped_2026_04_28):
- 8 stable steps
- Self-review checkpoint at start
- Rule 7 expanded to 5 registration modes
- Rule 9 (marketplace.json aspirational entries — verify on-disk presence)

**RULE 11 CANDIDATE (NEW — to codify if this bead succeeds)**: search broader than marketplaces and topic pages. Include:
- Personal dotfiles repos with `.claude/skills/` directories
- Idiosyncratic-naming personal-skills repos (sirius-cc-wu, InSelfControll, Nevaberry, dchuk patterns)
- Adjacent-host repos (Tauri-skills repos for transferable Rust-frontend patterns)
- Plugin-marketplace organization repos (pluginagentmarketplace pattern)
- Polymath/multi-domain repos with non-standard SKILL.md naming (rand/cc-polymath: INDEX.md + topic .md)
- Domain-specific ecosystems (blockchain WASM repos like multiversx)

**RULE 12 CANDIDATE**: non-standard SKILL.md naming. cc-polymath uses INDEX.md + topic-specific .md files instead of SKILL.md. The 8-step pre-flight (rule 1) currently requires SKILL.md; relax to 'a skill-content file at the leaf' and probe for INDEX.md as a fallback.

## Recommended starting candidates
The 17+ listed above. Start with Nevaberry@dioxus-knowledge-patch (TOP PICK — most current + MIT) + Daiki48@dioxus-guide (sibling for cross-validation).

## Output
.claude/plans/skill-comparison/discovery/rust-wasm-frontend-CORRIGENDUM.md per the standard discovery format. Include:
- Per-candidate verification table (slug + LOC + license + scope + registration mode)
- Per-domain picks (dioxus / leptos / yew / wasm-bindgen / trunk / rust-fullstack)
- Cross-validation note: which Phase 4 candidates are GENUINELY missing vs which were the sub-agent's search-pattern miss
- Methodology rule 11 + 12 promotion proposal with evidence
- Cross-reference to alphaonedev/openclaw-graph as a SEPARATE architecture-survey reference (compare to forge-ucc2/forge-afyg's petgraph approach)

ALSO update (this bead's parent agent does the file edits — sub-agent only writes the discovery output):
- rust-wasm-frontend.md: add a 'CORRIGENDUM' section pointing to this bead's output
- CONSOLIDATED.md: revise the 'Honest gap' framing for Dioxus + add corrigendum row
- clusters.md: revise the forge-qtzs Phase 4 entry; add rule 11 + rule 12 amendments
- Session-end memory: update with corrigendum reference + revised methodology

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] discovery/rust-wasm-frontend-CORRIGENDUM.md written
- [ ] All 17+ candidates verified (each: exists/license/LOC/scope/registration mode)
- [ ] Per-domain corrected pick recommendations
- [ ] alphaonedev/openclaw-graph cataloged as REFERENCE (architecture-survey, not install)
- [ ] Rule 11 + Rule 12 codification proposal with evidence
- [ ] Memory at session close
close_reason:
Corrigendum complete; output at .claude/plans/skill-comparison/discovery/rust-wasm-frontend-CORRIGENDUM.md (284 lines, 36958 bytes).

VERIFIED: 27 candidate rows (vs 17 pre-surfaced in bead description).
- 14/17 pre-surfaced VERIFIED (with caveats)
- 1 CONFIRMED-NOT-EXISTING: sirius-cc-wu@dioxus-stitch (hallucinated in bead description; verifier discipline applies even to bead descriptions themselves)
- 2 REDIRECTED: sirius-cc-wu/dioxus-ui-ux actually lives in separate Python repo dioxus-ui-skill (not sirius-skills); the original first-pass URL set caught the wrong repo
- 10+ NEW FINDS via Rule 11 sweep: Nevaberry's plugin marketplace ships 130+ knowledge patches (vendor-of-record-tier alongside HashiCorp) — net-new Rust/JS-frontend-relevant: tauri-knowledge-patch, axum-knowledge-patch, sqlx-knowledge-patch, tokio-knowledge-patch, serde-knowledge-patch, vite-knowledge-patch, htmx-knowledge-patch, svelte-knowledge-patch, tailwind-knowledge-patch, rust-knowledge-patch. Plus 4 additional Daiki48 dotfiles skills (axum-guide, sqlx-postgres, rusqlite-guide, gemini-search).

PER-DOMAIN CORRECTED PICKS:
- DIOXUS: Nevaberry@dioxus-knowledge-patch (TOP — MIT, 191 LOC, Dioxus 0.7.4, marketplace-installable, default branch MASTER)
- LEPTOS: Nevaberry@leptos-knowledge-patch (TOP — MIT, 47 LOC, Leptos 0.8.8-0.8.17)
- YEW: only dchuk@tauri-frontend-rust table-mention; NO standalone Yew skill exists (partial gap)
- WASM-BINDGEN: pluginagentmarketplace@rust-wasm (TOP — 316 LOC, Custom License, full coverage); secondary cc-polymath@wasm/wasm-rust-toolchain.md
- TRUNK: only dchuk@tauri-frontend-rust (Trunk-only bundler section); no standalone Trunk skill
- RUST-FULLSTACK: Daiki48@rust-fullstack (license-blocked); composable Nevaberry stack (MIT) is redistribution-clean alternative
- GENERAL WASM: cc-polymath@wasm bundle loaded via cc-polymath@discover-wasm gateway (Rule 12 trigger — INDEX.md+4 topic .md files, MIT, densest WASM coverage)

CROSS-VALIDATION (forge-qtzs gap claims):
- 0 of 6 hard 'zero' claims survive intact. All 6 are wrong or partial.
- 'DIY needed' list shrinks from 4 items (dioxus / wasm-bindgen / trunk / wa-sqlite) to 1.5 items.
- **wa-sqlite + IDBBatchAtomicVFS is the ONLY true authoring opportunity that survives** (no Claude skill exists anywhere).
- Trunk is a SOFT survivor (no standalone, only bundler context in dchuk).
- 'Forge is operating ahead of the public skill ecosystem' framing is WRONG for Dioxus (Nevaberry is current with 0.7.4) but CORRECT for wa-sqlite.

RULE 11 + 12 READY FOR STABLE PROMOTION:
- Rule 11: 6-surface specification (personal dotfiles / idiosyncratic personal-skills / adjacent-host / plugin-marketplace orgs / polymath / domain-specific ecosystems) + convergence criterion (probe ≥3 of 6 before declaring 'zero'). Validated by all 27 candidates spread across 6 surfaces.
- Rule 12: 4-pattern probe order (SKILL.md → INDEX.md+topic .md → README.md+AGENTS.md+data → upstream agents.md). Validated by cc-polymath wasm/rust bundles, sirius-cc-wu/dioxus-ui-skill, dioxus/AGENTS.md.
- Rule 13 SOFT CANDIDATE (do NOT promote yet): probe master as first-class default branch, not fallback. Nevaberry uses master. 4 confirmed master-default repos cumulatively; promote at ≥6.

OPENCLAW-GRAPH ARCHITECTURE-SURVEY DETAIL:
- 316 Skill nodes / 27 SkillCluster nodes / 217 RELATED_TO edges / 316 IN_CLUSTER edges, MIT, hybrid Python+Rust+TS+Shell.
- neo4j-sync Rust daemon: 3.8 MB arm64 binary, ~4 MB RSS, ~5ms per-sync vs 555ms Python (≈100x speedup), <500ms FSEvents-driven write-back, <1ms skill lookup.
- Compaction story: workspace stubs reduced from 11k-25k bytes to ~655 bytes total across 5 files.
- 6 cross-comparison rows vs Forge's SkillGraphRuntime (forge-ucc2 + forge-afyg) written for future cross-architecture doc.
- Direct quantitative evidence for Forge's 'zero subprocess calls / Rust rewrite' stance (100x speedup over Python polling).

SURPRISES:
- sirius-cc-wu@dioxus-stitch was HALLUCINATED in bead description's pre-surfaced list — zero matches anywhere under that user. Verifier discipline applies even to bead descriptions themselves.
- Default-branch matters AGAIN: Nevaberry uses master — initial 8-step pre-flight on main returned 404s for ALL probes. Strongest argument yet for Rule 13.
- Nevaberry's marketplace is FAR RICHER than bead surfaced — 130+ knowledge patches across nearly every modern Rust/JS/Python framework. Vendor-of-record-tier alongside HashiCorp. Deserves dedicated audit pass in future bead.
- License pattern: dotfiles-published almost UNIVERSALLY lack LICENSE (Daiki48: no license; sirius-cc-wu/dioxus-ui-skill: no license). For redistribution-clean alternatives, prefer MARKETPLACE-registered authors (Nevaberry MIT, multiversx MIT, mohitmishra786 MIT).
- Rule 12 evidence STRONGER than anticipated: cc-polymath has 31 gateways (skills using INDEX.md+topic .md pattern) across 30 categories, not just the wasm bundle.
- pluginagentmarketplace org has 70 repos (custom-plugin-rust + custom-plugin-java + custom-plugin-swift + custom-plugin-game-developer + ...) — org pattern is real and worth dedicated sweep bead.

SELF-REVIEW: NOT triggered (27 ≤ 30 threshold). No decomposition. If future Phase-N expands Rule 11 surfaces 5-6 in depth (Substrate / NEAR / wasmtime ecosystem pass), per-framework sub-bead decomposition becomes appropriate.

Memory at session close per AC.

title: Terraform: design drift-detection workflow (cron, alerting, dedup)
description: Sibling of forge-xk0z.21 (TF state backend design). Drift detection was deliberately deferred from the state-backend spec because it warrants its own design pass. Scope: a tf-drift.yml GH Action that runs scheduled tofu plan against both terraform/cf/ and terraform/github/ states, detects out-of-band changes, and surfaces them. Substantive open questions: cron cadence (twice-daily? hourly? on-demand?), alerting channel (GH issue with dedup? email? Slack? — note that GH Actions can't write to local Dolt, so beads-issue creation isn't directly possible), how to dedup so the same drift doesn't re-spam, and whether to auto-correct trivial drift (e.g., display_name changes) vs always require human review. Output: a short design spec under .claude/plans/forge-terraform-bootstrap/refs/specs/ once xk0z.21 lands. Blocked on xk0z.21 implementation (state must exist before drift can be detected).

title: Discover: Payments (Stripe + alternatives)
description:
## Context
Forge SaaS needs payments. User leaning Stripe; needs to lock-in skill choice before that decision. Adjacent: subscription billing, checkout flows, customer portal.

## Domains
- stripe (primary — user's lean)
- payments (general)
- billing
- subscriptions
- paddle (alternative — Stripe's main competitor for SaaS)
- lemonsqueezy (alternative — Merchant of Record model, common for indie SaaS)

## Search-term matrix
| Domain | Tier 1 | Tier 2 | Tier 3 | Operators |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| stripe | "stripe" | "stripe checkout", "stripe billing", "stripe subscriptions" | "stripe webhook" | path:.claude-plugin |
| payments | "payments" | "payment processor", "merchant" | "transaction" | path:.claude-plugin |
| paddle | "paddle.com", "paddle billing" | "paddle subscriptions" | — | — |
| lemonsqueezy | "lemonsqueezy" | "lemon squeezy" | "indie SaaS payments" | — |

title: Skill discovery for Forge SaaS — harvest candidates for Phase 4-8 comparisons
description:
## Context

Forge SaaS stack (Astro + Dioxus/Rust→WASM + CF Pages/Workers/D1/R2/KV + Stripe + Terraform + MDBook + GitHub CI/CD + UI/UX work + site audit) opens 15+ new domains where skill candidates haven't been catalogued. This epic harvests candidates so they can be promoted to comparison beads.

## Children (10)
- CF stack remainder (Pages + KV)
- Rust/WASM frontend (Dioxus + sibling frameworks)
- Payments (Stripe + alternatives)
- IaC + CI/CD + provenance (Terraform-CF + GHA + build-attestation pull-forward)
- UI primitives (icon, color-palette, design-extract)
- UX flows (onboarding, auth, profile, ux-patterns)
- Assets + library survey (image/SVG gen + UI library surveys)

- Documentation (mdbook, diataxis, technical-writing)
- Trademark/copyright follow-up (EU/UKIPO/Madrid/copyright/DMCA)
- Tier 3 sweep / pull-forward audit (depends on the discovery children closing first)

## Methodology constants (every child)
1. Apply 8-step pre-flight from prior phase memories
2. Read-only via WebFetch + npx skills find — no installs
3. Self-review checkpoint at start: scan candidate space; if >20 plausible skills OR >5 distinct sub-patterns emerge, STOP and create sub-beads to decompose before executing further

## Epic-close consolidation (parent's job)
After all children close, write `.claude/plans/skill-comparison/discovery/CONSOLIDATED.md` — a single index + cross-cluster summary table linking each child's output and rolling up: total clusters discovered, ≥2-member candidates ready for comparison beads, singletons recommended for direct install, gaps where DIY is required.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 10 child beads closed
- [ ] discovery/CONSOLIDATED.md aggregates all child outputs into a navigable index + cross-cluster summary
- [ ] Capture work as Memory at epic close
close_reason:

- assets-library-survey.md (forge-fouu) — jezweb dominant; Stock-image GAP; Svelte components EFFECTIVE GAP; Dioxus CONFIRMED ZERO; secondsky ~50% ghost rate (rule #9 candidate)
- site-quality.md (forge-0sz1) — Self-review HIT 3/5 sub-domains; jdevalk+ArtSabintsev+openairlabs lean GEO ensemble; NLWeb is jdevalk moat; recommends retire-the-framing

METHODOLOGY CODIFICATION (now 8 stable + 2 amendments + 1 candidate):
- Rule 7 EXPANSION: registration mode is now 5 options — marketplace.json / SKILLS.md / folder-published / CLI-installer (npx) / codex-plugin/plugin.json. ~half of surveyed Phase 4 skills cannot use /plugin install.
- Rule 9 NEW: marketplace.json entries can be ASPIRATIONAL without folder backing (secondsky ~230/400 ghost rate; PostHog mis-coverage). Verify on-disk presence per slug.
- Rule 10 SOFT CANDIDATE: audit external CLI/vendor-binary dep licenses, not just skill license (squirrelscan wraps closed-source squirrel CLI). Promote at ≥2 cases.

KEY HEADLINES:

Optional pair with alinaqi/claude-bootstrap@cloudflare-d1 if Forge commits to Drizzle ORM + multi-tenant DB-per-customer. Avoid jezweb (too narrow — ships d1-drizzle-schema + d1-migration as separate slugs, not a unified cloudflare-d1). title: Compare 'cloudflare-r2' cluster (6 skills, Tier 1)
description:
## Why
Six third-party agent skills share the slug 'cloudflare-r2'. Direct value for forge-4a01 (CF Browser-First Deployment, MVP 2.0 ship target). Choosing among them blocks any plan that uses R2 for snapshot storage.

## Cluster members
1. jezweb/claude-skills@cloudflare-r2
2. ovachiever/droid-tings@cloudflare-r2
3. jackspace/claudeskillz@cloudflare-r2
4. bagelhole/devops-security-agent-skills@cloudflare-r2
5. membranedev/application-skills@cloudflare-r2
6. itechmeat/llm-code@cloudflare-r2

## Methodology
- Read-only via WebFetch against GitHub. No 'npx skills add' installs.
- Score against Template A General matrix + R2 sub-aspect coverage matrix.
- Tag subjective columns [judgment]. Reproducible columns (size, prereqs, license, examples) are objective.
- Mark inaccessible skills 'not accessible: <reason>' and continue.

## Reference
- .claude/plans/skill-comparison/templates.md (Template A.1 + A.2 cloudflare-r2 sub-aspects)
- Parent epic: forge-2obz

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 6 skills assessed (or marked not accessible)
- [ ] Both matrices filled (Template A General + R2 sub-aspect coverage)
- [ ] Summary recommendations section ('best general fit', 'best for specific X', 'avoid', 'gaps')
- [ ] Capture work as a Memory at session close (per bd convention)

Recommendation for forge-4a01: pair jackspace@cloudflare-r2 (Workers-runtime + presigned + multipart, 1175 lines) with bagelhole@cloudflare-r2 (lifecycle + S3-SDK + public buckets, 337 lines) for full coverage. Critical findings: (1) jezweb and itechmeat do NOT ship cloudflare-r2 — cluster registry mis-tagged 33% of members, needs correction in clusters.md; (2) ovachiever and jackspace appear to be near-identical content (same structure/code/'Last Updated' stamp ~27KB each) — treat as duplicates, suspect derivation; (3) membranedev is a Membrane-platform shim with broad activation triggers, recommended to avoid; (4) ZERO coverage of R2 event notifications across the cluster — gap if Forge needs change-tracking on uploaded assets; (5) Workers-side skills skip lifecycle policies entirely, only bagelhole covers them. Methodology caveat: WebFetch can confabulate plausible SKILL.md content for 404'd URLs — verify via plugin manifest/directory listing.

title: Terraform: design TF state backend (security, defense-in-depth, deploy flow, bootstrap)
description:
Before any Terraform module is written for this repo, we need a deliberate decision on where TF state lives and how it's secured + accessed. Initial discussion 2026-04-28 surfaced R2 + native locking (TF ≥ 1.10) as the leading candidate, but security and defense-in-depth need real consideration before commit.

## Dimensions to design

### Backend selection

| Backend | Native lock | Vendor footprint | Free tier |
|---------|-------------|------------------|-----------|
| R2 (S3-compat) | TF ≥ 1.10 | Cloudflare (already in stack) | Yes |
| HCP Terraform | Yes | HashiCorp | Yes (small) |
| S3 + DynamoDB | Yes | AWS | Yes (small) |
| Local | No | None | — |

### Security

- State files contain plaintext resource IDs, sometimes secret material (sensitive values get redacted in newer providers but not all). Treat state as secret regardless.
- Encryption at rest: R2 has it; HCP has it; S3 needs SSE-KMS.
- Access control: who can read state? Compromise of state = compromise of resources it describes.
- Audit: who applied what, when?

### Defense in depth

- Separate TF state from runtime resource credentials (state-reader role ≠ resource-mutator role).
- Lock leakage: a stale lock should fail loud, not silently allow concurrent applies.
- State-history retention: how long do we keep old state versions for rollback / forensics?
- Backup: R2 versioning enabled?

### Deploy flow

- Who applies TF? Operator from laptop? CI? Both with different scopes?
- Plan-vs-apply gating: human review of plan output required before apply?
- Drift detection: scheduled `terraform plan` to alert on out-of-band changes?
- Secret injection: TF variables sourced from CF Secrets Store, env, sops-encrypted file?

### Bootstrap

- The state backend itself can't be in TF state (chicken/egg). Bootstrap is one-time: create the R2 bucket (or HCP workspace) via dashboard or wrangler.
- Document the bootstrap procedure in docs/src/operations/ (when that page exists).

## Output

A short design spec under `.claude/plans/forge-terraform-bootstrap/` covering:
- Backend pick + rationale
- IAM / token model (separation of plan-read vs apply-mutate)
- Apply mechanism (laptop vs CI, with what gates)
- Bootstrap procedure
- Migration path if we change backends later

Plus: any beads filed for the actual implementation (R2 bucket creation, TF version pin, CI workflow, etc.).

## Blocked on

Nothing — this is design work. But forge-xk0z.??? (terraform/ contents brainstorm) should ideally land in parallel since the two inform each other.

## References

- forge-xk0z.14 — CORS Transform Rules migration; consumer of TF once it exists.
- forge-xk0z.8 — rate-limit WAF rules; consumer of TF.
notes:
2026-04-29: implementation beads filed.

Phase 0 + Phase 1.1-1.3 implementation beads did not exist as of 2026-04-29 despite spec approval on 2026-04-28. Filed:

- forge-dfwx — Phase 0 manual prerequisites (1Password vault + 4 Environments, CF tokens, R2 bucket + Object Versioning, R2 access keys, 1Password Service Account, 4 GH Environments + 2 secrets each).
- forge-f6i1 — Phase 1.1-1.3 laptop bootstrap (terraform/cf/ skeleton HCL, .opentofu-version pin, .gitignore += .envrc, tofu init, cloudflare_r2_bucket.tf_state import with prevent_destroy).

Wired deps: this bead now depends on both. Closes when f6i1 closes (which itself blocks on dfwx). Phase 2 (CI cutover) and Phase 1.4 (resource imports) remain xk0z.24 and xk0z.23+ territory respectively.

forge-cw83 (load-secrets-action v1 / SHA pin) remains a hard prereq for Phase 2 but is not blocking for Phase 0 or Phase 1.1-1.3 (laptop-only at that stage).

title: Terraform: brainstorm + plan terraform/ contents (which CF resources land in TF, in what order)
description:
Companion to the TF state design bead. Once the state backend is chosen, the question becomes: what resources go in TF, in what order, and how is the codebase laid out?

## Hard split principle (from session discussion 2026-04-28)

TF manages **resources** (DNS, projects, bindings, rules). Tool-native (`wrangler`, Pages auto-build, Workers Builds) ships **content** (Worker code, site assets, mdBook pages).

Worker code lives in repo; the Worker resource (the slot it lives in, its bindings, its routes) lives in TF. Same for Pages projects.

## Initial inventory (verdicts pending design refinement)

| Resource | Verdict | Notes |
|----------|---------|-------|
| DNS records (resu.mx zone, subdomains) | Yes | Foundational; everything else routes through this |
| Pages projects (forge-infra-landing, future forge-infra-docs) | Yes | TF resource exists |
| Workers (forge-auth) — bindings/routes/secret-shape | Discuss | Couples Worker code (repo) to deploy state (TF); see "hard split" |
| D1 / KV / R2 resources | Yes for creation; binding question same as Workers |
| WAF rules / Transform Rules (xk0z.8 rate-limit, xk0z.14 CORS) | Yes | Perfect TF fit |
| CF Access policies | Yes | When we have gated surfaces |
| GitHub repo / branch protection / secrets | Maybe | Adds GH credential management; requires `github` provider |
| GitHub OAuth App config | No | Provider support shallow |

## Module structure to design

- `terraform/modules/dns-resu-mx/` — zone config, records
- `terraform/modules/cf-pages/` — reusable Pages project
- `terraform/modules/cf-worker/` — reusable Worker resource (slot, not code)
- `terraform/modules/cf-d1/` — D1 + bindings
- `terraform/modules/cf-kv/` — KV + bindings
- `terraform/envs/prod/` — root module composing the above

Or flatter — single root, no envs split — at PoC scale.

## Maturity gates (in what order to TF things)

1. **Bootstrap** (after state design lands): R2 bucket for state, TF backend config, first `terraform apply` of nothing-yet, prove the loop.
2. **DNS records** (lowest risk to import): document existing DNS, then `terraform import` resu.mx zone records into TF.
3. **Pages projects**: import existing forge-infra-landing.
4. **WAF / Transform Rules**: greenfield, no import needed (xk0z.14 CORS, xk0z.8 rate-limit are perfect first writes).
5. **Workers / D1 / KV / R2**: gradual import, paired with the "hard split" question.
6. **GitHub / Access / Zero Trust**: after the above is stable.

## Output

Spec under `.claude/plans/forge-terraform-bootstrap/refs/specs/` covering: module layout, resource priority order, import-vs-greenfield strategy per resource type, naming conventions for TF identifiers, secret variable conventions.

Plus: implementation beads for each maturity gate.

## Out of scope

- Picking the state backend (separate bead).
- Writing the TF code (this bead is design only; implementation beads spawn from it).

## References

- Companion bead: TF state backend design.
- forge-xk0z.14 — first concrete consumer of TF (CORS Transform Rules).
- forge-xk0z.8 — second concrete consumer (WAF rate-limit rules).
notes:
2026-04-28: naming-convention update + import strategy clarification.

## Naming
Per session decision (workers.md convention change), CF resources follow `resumx-<role>`:
- Worker: `resumx-auth` (renamed from `forge-auth` in the same session)
- Pages: `resumx-landing` (renamed from `forge-infra-landing` in the same session); future `resumx-docs`
- D1: `resumx-auth-db` (renamed from `forge-auth-db`)
- KV: `resumx-auth-kv` (renamed from `forge-auth-kv`)

Update the inventory table in the original description to use these names when this bead is worked.

## Import-first strategy

The `resumx-*` resources will exist (created via `wrangler` / dashboard) BEFORE TF lands. When TF code is written, the strategy for these existing resources is **import**, not create:

1. Bootstrap TF state backend (per xk0z.21).
2. Write resource definitions in TF that match the live resources (right names, right bindings, right routes).
3. `terraform import` each existing resource into TF state.
4. Run `terraform plan` — should show zero diff if the TF code matches reality.
5. Iterate until plan is clean.
6. From this point, TF is the source of truth; manual changes are drift.

Import order (lowest risk first):
1. DNS records (resu.mx zone) — no functional change, just bringing config under TF.
2. Pages project (`resumx-landing`) — same.
3. D1 / KV resources (`resumx-auth-db`, `resumx-auth-kv`) — resource only, not data.
4. Worker (`resumx-auth`) — slot only (bindings, routes, secrets-shape); Worker code stays in `workers/auth/src/` and ships via Workers Builds, NOT TF.

## Greenfield (NOT import) — for resources that don't exist yet
- Future WAF rules / Transform Rules (xk0z.8 rate-limit, xk0z.14 CORS migration).
- Future CF Access policies.
- Future `resumx-docs` Pages project (if not created manually first).

2026-04-29: design spec drafted, self-reviewed, awaiting human gate.

Spec: .claude/plans/forge-terraform-bootstrap/refs/specs/2026-04-29-terraform-contents-design.md

Key locked decisions (Q1-Q7 from brainstorm):
- Q1 Hard split for Workers = B. TF owns DNS + custom_domain + routes + raw D1/KV/R2 resources. Wrangler owns bindings + vars + secrets-shape + code. cloudflare_workers_script is NEVER imported into TF; the Worker name string is the cross-tool contract.
- Q2 Module structure = A (flat, file-split-by-resource-type). Module Lifecycle stages: in-repo flat (today) → in-repo private module (signal: second instance + ≥70% duplication) → out-of-repo monorepo with tag prefixes (signal: cross-repo consumption). Submodule pattern deferred to forge-sxdl (filed during this session).
- Q3 GH side scope = B+ (rulesets + Environments + env-vars + repo settings + CODEOWNERS for workflow YAML, the latter as a code file not TF).
- Q4 Resource priority = bead's order (DNS → Pages → D1/KV → Worker → WAF/Transform) + B (per-resource dry-run pattern documented as Phase 1.4z, applied to every import).
- Q5 Ruleset content = B (branch + push + tag rulesets, no commit-message regex; 50 MB push cap; required-deployment rule deferred to a follow-up step to avoid chicken-and-egg). github_repository in scope.
- Q6 Implementation breakdown = A (six spawn beads): Phase 1.4a (DNS) → Phase 2 (CI cutover) → Phase 1.4b (Pages) + Phase 1.4c (D1+KV) + Phase 1.4d (Worker) + Phase 3 (GH side full).
- Q7 Worker slot interpretation = (b) routing-layer only. TF doesn't touch cloudflare_workers_script; only cloudflare_workers_custom_domain + cloudflare_dns_record. Contract is the Worker name string.

Self-review pass via superpowers:code-reviewer raised 1 critical, 3 high, 4 medium, 7 low findings. All C/H/M and most L addressed in revision pass. Three Open Questions parked for verify-at-implementation: custom_domain attribute name (script vs service in v5), custom_domain import ID format, github ruleset target syntax shape.

Filed during this session: forge-sxdl (revisit monorepo-of-modules with submodule pattern; depends on this bead).

Bead remains OPEN pending human approval. Implementation beads spawn after approval; this bead closes when the six spawn beads close.

2026-04-29: design spec approved by aRustyDev. Bead status reverted to OPEN; awaiting implementation.

Spawned beads (under forge-xk0z initiative):
- forge-xk0z.23 — Phase 1.4a DNS imports (depends on xk0z.21 implementation)
- forge-xk0z.24 — Phase 2 CI cutover (depends on xk0z.23)
- forge-xk0z.25 — Phase 1.4b Pages import (depends on xk0z.24)
- forge-xk0z.26 — Phase 1.4c D1+KV imports (depends on xk0z.24)
- forge-xk0z.27 — Phase 1.4d Worker custom_domain rolling-cut (depends on xk0z.24, xk0z.23)
- forge-xk0z.28 — Phase 3 GH side full (depends on xk0z.24)

Existing dependent beads now wired:
- forge-xk0z.8 (rate-limit WAF) — blocked by xk0z.27
- forge-xk0z.14 (CORS Transform Rules) — blocked by xk0z.27

This bead (xk0z.22) closes when xk0z.23-.28 all close, not at spec approval.

title: Auth: enable Workers Builds for forge-auth (one-time dashboard step)
description:
The Cloudflare GitHub App is authorized account-wide, but Workers Builds is **not turned on for the forge-auth Worker specifically** (verified via mcp__cf-workers-build__workers_builds_list_builds → total_count: 0). Until enabled, every Worker change requires manual `cd workers/auth && just deploy` from a developer's laptop.

## Action

Open Cloudflare dashboard → Workers & Pages → forge-auth → Settings → Builds. Connect the GitHub repo (aRustyDev/forge-infra). Configure:
- Branch: `main`
- Build command: `cd workers/auth && npm ci && npx tsc --noEmit`  (the build itself; CF runs `wrangler deploy` afterward)
- Root directory: leave at repo root, or set to `workers/auth/` if CF supports subdir builds (verify in dashboard)
- Auto-deploy on push to main: enable

## Acceptance criteria

- A push to main that touches `workers/auth/**` triggers a Workers Build visible in `mcp__cf-workers-build__workers_builds_list_builds`.
- The deployed Worker's `Current Version ID` updates after a no-op push (e.g., a comment edit in `auth.ts`).
- `cd workers/auth && just deploy` continues to work as a manual override / fallback.

## Out of scope

- Migrating the manual `just deploy` flow away (keep as an operator escape hatch).
- Smoke tests (those go in `.github/workflows/smoke.yml`, independent of deploy path).

## References

- `.claude/rules/workers.md` Deploy & CI table — captures the three deploy paths and why CF-native is the default for plain deploys.
close_reason: Satisfied by the resumx-auth migration. Workers Builds was enabled for resumx-auth (the renamed Worker, ID 66c24ff097e84a53a76a7bfd607aca33) on 2026-04-28 as part of Phase E of the rename. The original bead was filed against forge-auth, which has since been deleted as part of the same migration. Workers Builds for resumx-auth has 0 builds yet (no commit has touched workers/auth/** since the dashboard step); next workers/auth change will validate the auto-deploy path end-to-end.

title: forge_align_resume scores against perspective text, not entry override content
description: forge_align_resume embeds and compares each requirement against perspective.content (or perspective_content_snapshot) rather than the resume_entry.content override when one is present. This means tailoring an entry via forge_update_resume_entry with sharper resume-specific text has zero effect on alignment scores — the engine still uses the original perspective text. Repro: take any resume entry where entry.content differs from perspective.content; run align; observe that requirement_matches[].best_match.perspective_content reflects the perspective text, not the override. Expected: when entry.content is set, the alignment engine should embed the override and compare against it. unmatched_entries also lists pre-override text. Impact: makes JD-tailored override content invisible to alignment scoring, defeating one of the main reasons to use overrides.

title: MCP API: no transition from 'draft' to 'pending_review' for perspectives/bullets
description: forge_create_perspective with auto_approve=false creates a perspective in 'draft' status. The only state transitions exposed via MCP are pending_review→approved (forge_approve_perspective), pending_review→rejected (forge_reject_perspective), and rejected→pending_review (forge_reopen_perspective). There is no MCP-exposed path to move a draft into pending_review or directly to approved without recreating with auto_approve=true. This makes the 'draft' state a dead-end for any agent-driven workflow that wants human review before approval. Same gap likely exists for bullets. Add forge_submit_perspective and forge_submit_bullet (draft→pending_review), or extend forge_approve_* to accept draft as input.

title: Kanban perspective card click renders source bullet in modal, not the perspective
description: In the perspective kanban/board view, clicking a perspective card opens the modal but populates it with the bullet that the perspective was derived from rather than the perspective itself. Likely a wiring error where the click handler passes bullet_id instead of perspective_id, or the modal data fetcher is following the bullet provenance link instead of fetching the perspective by id. Fix should ensure perspective fields (content, domain, framing, status, target_archetype, approved_at, approved_by) are what render — not the underlying bullet's content_snapshot.

title: Add 'status' dropdown to perspective/bullet edit modal
description: The perspective and bullet edit modals currently expose no way to change status (draft / pending_review / approved / rejected) — the only path is the kanban DnD UI. Add a 'status' dropdown to both modals so users can change status from within the modal in addition to via DnD. Reuse the same status enum the kanban columns are derived from. The DnD path must remain functional after this change. Acceptance: dropdown wired to same mutation as DnD, optimistic update reflected in any open kanban view, no regression in kanban column rendering.

title: Kanban view defaults to 'bullets' on reload, ignoring 'perspectives' selection
description: In the kanban/board view, the entity-type toggle (bullets vs perspectives) does not persist across page reloads. Reloading always returns the user to the 'bullets' view even if they had selected 'perspectives'. Likely a missing localStorage/URL-query persistence on the toggle state. Should also restore selected status filters and column scroll positions where reasonable.

title: Close workspace hygiene workarounds (commit template.rs / forge-server lib.rs / packages/justfile)
description:
## Why

forge-lu5s landed three reversible workarounds because main HEAD has
manifests pointing at files that the maintainer holds locally but
hasn't committed:

- **forge-es6o** — `crates/forge-sdk/src/db/stores/mod.rs` declares
  `pub mod template;` and `pub use template::TemplateStore;`. The
  workaround (commit `fc13e74` on branch `forge-lu5s`) commented both
  lines.
- **forge-2qns** — `crates/forge-server/Cargo.toml` declares a `[lib]`
  target pointing at `src/lib.rs`. The workaround (commit `8745049`
  on branch `forge-lu5s`) commented out the entire `[lib]` block.
- **forge-6t8v** — root `justfile` declares `mod test "packages/justfile"`
  but `packages/justfile` is untracked. No workaround landed for this
  one (just-recipe paths still fail in fresh worktrees); will be fixed
  by committing `packages/justfile` here.

Each workaround restored fresh-checkout buildability for forge-lu5s's
`cargo check --workspace` gate, but they shadow real code that exists
locally on the maintainer's tree. The right fix is to commit the
actual files and revert the workarounds in one coordinated change.

## What's still untracked on main HEAD

```
crates/forge-sdk/src/db/stores/template.rs
crates/forge-server/src/db.rs
crates/forge-server/src/lib.rs
crates/forge-server/src/response.rs
crates/forge-server/src/state.rs
crates/forge-server/src/routes/templates.rs
crates/forge-server/tests/
packages/justfile
```

Plus modified-but-uncommitted (likely siblings of the lib split):
```
crates/forge-server/src/main.rs
crates/forge-server/src/routes/contacts.rs
```

## Approach

1. Branch from current `main` (post-cleanup commits 331a7b8..0383d3c).
2. Commit the three groups of files together with clear messages:
   - SDK: `template.rs` + uncomment lines in `db/stores/mod.rs`.
   - Server: `lib.rs` + siblings + uncomment `[lib]` in `Cargo.toml`
     + reconcile with maintainer's `main.rs` / `routes/contacts.rs`
     edits.
   - Tooling: `packages/justfile`.
3. Verify `cargo check --workspace` clean from a fresh worktree.
4. Verify `cargo test --workspace` green at expected baselines (forge-sdk
   should jump from 281 back up to 290+ once the template module
   re-compiles its tests).
5. Push + open PR; close referenced beads.

## Out of scope

- Don't touch `forge-lu5s` history — that branch is shipped/closed.
- Don't bundle unrelated cleanup. This bead is strictly about the
  three pre-existing workarounds.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `crates/forge-sdk/src/db/stores/template.rs` committed; `pub mod template;` and `pub use template::TemplateStore;` in `mod.rs` uncommented.
- [ ] `crates/forge-server/src/{lib.rs, db.rs, response.rs, state.rs, routes/templates.rs, tests/}` committed; `[lib]` block in `crates/forge-server/Cargo.toml` uncommented.
- [ ] `packages/justfile` committed; `just --list` works from a fresh worktree.
- [ ] `cargo check --workspace` clean from a fresh checkout of the post-bead main.
- [ ] `cargo test --workspace` green; forge-sdk count >= 281 (likely 290+ once template module re-includes its tests).
- [ ] Maintainer's pending edits to `crates/forge-server/src/main.rs` and `crates/forge-server/src/routes/contacts.rs` reconciled — either committed alongside `lib.rs` (likely same lib-split work) or rebased independently.
- [ ] Capture the work done in this session as a Memory, including what landed, key decisions encoded, lessons, and critical-path state. Save under /Users/adam/.claude/projects/-Users-adam-code-proj-forge/memory/ and update MEMORY.md index.

## References

- Spec: `.claude/plans/forge-resume-builder/refs/specs/2026-04-28-browserstore-adapter-vertical-slice.md` (section 5: Workspace hygiene fold-in)
- forge-lu5s commits: `fc13e74` (es6o), `8745049` (2qns)
- Closed-as-workaround: forge-es6o, forge-2qns (closed 2026-04-28)
- Open: forge-6t8v (still open; should close as part of this bead)

title: Auth flow demo page on resu.mx — sign-up, sign-in, sign-out, delete-account (PoC, idempotent)
description:
Add a demo page to the landing site (`sites/landing/`) that exercises the four core auth flows against the auth Worker at auth.resu.mx. Deliberately simple: HTML + minimal JS, no styling beyond the existing inline CSS, no Astro layout/components beyond what's needed for the page to render. Goal: prove the auth round-trip works E2E from a different origin in the same .resu.mx zone.

## Scope (in)

Page route: TBD — likely `/auth-demo`, `/poc`, or `/_dev/auth`. Hidden-ish (not in nav), but indexable for now (PoC page; we can robots-noindex later).

Four buttons + response panel:
1. **Sign up / Sign in (GitHub)** — same flow; redirects to `auth.resu.mx/api/auth/sign-in/social/github`. After return, page shows `/api/me` JSON.
2. **Sign out** — POST to `auth.resu.mx/api/auth/sign-out` with `credentials: 'include'`. Display response status. Hitting again when already signed out should be a no-op (idempotent).
3. **Delete account** — POST to `auth.resu.mx/api/auth/delete-user` (BetterAuth's endpoint; verify exact path during impl). Display response. Repeated call after delete should report user-not-found cleanly.
4. **Show me** — GET `auth.resu.mx/api/me` to display current session state. Idempotent.

Page also displays:
- The currently-set session cookie (parsed client-side; HttpOnly cookies won't be JS-readable, but the cookie's existence/absence influences subsequent fetches)
- The last response body (JSON pretty-printed) and status code

## Scope (out)

- Visual design / branding / Astro components beyond what makes the page render — that's downstream UI/templating work
- Idempotency for ALL edge cases — only the four flows above need to be safe to repeat
- Testing harness — manual exercise is sufficient at PoC stage
- Email-based delete confirmation (BetterAuth's default flow may require a confirmation token from email; if so, document the workaround as a simpler 'force-delete admin endpoint' or skip until email infra exists)

## Sub-tasks within this bead

- **CORS on the auth Worker**: `resu.mx` and `auth.resu.mx` are different origins; fetch calls from the demo page need CORS allowance + `credentials: 'include'` on the client + proper Access-Control-Allow-Origin/Credentials headers from the Worker. BetterAuth has `trustedOrigins` config; verify it sets the right CORS headers. If not, add explicit middleware in the Worker.
- **Cookie domain verification**: The auth Worker sets cookies with `Domain=.resu.mx`. Verify they're sent on fetch from `resu.mx` to `auth.resu.mx`.

## Dependencies

**BLOCKED ON forge-xk0z.12** (BetterAuth 1.6.9 HTTP handler returns 404 for /api/auth/* paths). Without fixing that, all flows on this demo page will hit 404. Don't start UI implementation until forge-xk0z.12 closes.

## Files affected (anticipated)

- `infra/sites/landing/src/pages/<demo-route>.astro` — the page itself
- `infra/sites/landing/src/pages/<demo-route>.css` (optional, inline likely)
- `infra/workers/auth/src/auth.ts` — possibly add `trustedOrigins` if needed for CORS
- `infra/workers/auth/src/index.ts` — possibly add CORS middleware if BetterAuth's built-in is insufficient

## Spec / plan

No separate spec needed — scope is small and well-bounded by this bead. Write the implementation plan inline when work starts.

Tracking the broader auth surface: forge-xk0z (Infrastructure → Operations theme).
notes:
2026-04-28: implementation done; awaiting browser E2E by aRustyDev.

## Worker side (commit 96e1a82, deployed a2bda346)
- CORS middleware in src/index.ts: OPTIONS preflight + response decoration with origin allowlist (RESUMX_AUTH_ALLOWED_ORIGINS).
- user.deleteUser.enabled = true in src/auth.ts.
- All 7 CORS smoke tests pass; delete-user enabled flag confirmed (401, not 404).

## Page side (commit pending push)
- sites/landing/src/pages/dev/auth.astro — single static page, 6 buttons, response panel, auto-/api/me on load.
- Live at https://resu.mx/dev/auth/ (deployed via direct Pages deploy, see deploy quirk below).

## Browser E2E checklist (for the human)
1. Open https://resu.mx/dev/auth/ — page loads, "Last response" auto-shows /api/me 401.
2. Click "Sign in (GitHub)" — redirects to GitHub OAuth, accept, land back on /dev/auth.
3. Confirm /api/me now returns user JSON with status 200.
4. Click "Sign out" — 200, cookie cleared.
5. Click "Show me" — /api/me returns 401.
6. Click "Sign up (GitHub)" — same flow as sign-in (different label, same endpoint); confirms callback round-trip.
7. Click "Delete account" — confirm dialog, then 200 with {success:true,message:"User deleted"} (session must be fresh, default 24h).
8. Click "Show me" — /api/me 401 (deleted user has no session).

## Deploy quirk (filed as xk0z.17)
The GH auto-build for commit 96e1a82 failed. Bypassed with direct `wrangler pages deploy dist --project-name forge-infra-landing` to get /dev/auth/ live for browser testing. xk0z.17 covers investigation + reconciliation of the Workers-Assets-vs-Pages config drift.

## Follow-up beads filed
- forge-xk0z.14: move CORS to CF Transform Rules (after Terraform module exists)
- forge-xk0z.15: revisit GitHub OAuth App constraints + optimizations
- forge-xk0z.16: review auth-demo JS shape + patterns
- forge-xk0z.17: investigate failing GH auto-build + reconcile deploy config

Close after browser E2E succeeds.

Worker side: CORS middleware + delete-user enabled. Implementation pushed in commit fa37e96. Follow-ups tracked under forge-xk0z.14 (CORS→Transform Rules), forge-xk0z.15 (GitHub OAuth audit), forge-xk0z.16 (JS shape review), forge-xk0z.17 (Pages auto-build + Workers/Pages drift).

title: BetterAuth 1.6.9 HTTP handler returns 404 for all /api/auth/* paths
description:
## Symptom

forge-auth Worker (commit 18d08d4 + later experiments) returns HTTP 404 with content-length: 0 for ALL /api/auth/* paths despite a successful deploy and bound bindings/secrets.

Reproduction:
  curl -i https://auth.resu.mx/api/auth/ok          → 404
  curl -i https://auth.resu.mx/api/auth/get-session → 404
  curl -i https://auth.resu.mx/api/auth/sign-in/social/github → 404

Worker tail confirms requests reach the handler and BetterAuth returns the 404 (no exception thrown). Even the simplest registered route /ok (registered in node_modules/better-auth/dist/api/routes/ok.mjs as createAuthEndpoint('/ok', { method: 'GET', ... })) is unreachable.

## What works

- Worker deploys cleanly to auth.resu.mx (custom_domain via routes block)
- D1 (forge-auth-db, id 2a6cae73-fe89-46cb-bbce-55427341c9d9) created and BetterAuth's 4 tables present (user, session, account, verification)
- KV (forge-auth-kv, id da262069362b4f798fc4d6914fcbd212) bound
- Secrets bound: RESUMX_BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, RESUMX_GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET (via wrangler secret list)
- Var bound: RESUMX_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID (in wrangler.jsonc vars)
- BetterAuth API methods work: /api/me calls auth(env).api.getSession() and correctly returns 401 when no cookie (proves init is fine, schema readable)
- Worker debug logs (console.log added during diagnosis) show our fetch handler runs, URL strip works, BetterAuth handler is called
- BetterAuth init does NOT throw — failure is silent 404

## Root-cause analysis (suspected)

In node_modules/@better-auth/core/dist/context/create-context.mjs around line 88, the init transforms options:

  options = {
    ...options,
    secret,
    baseURL: isDynamicConfig ? options.baseURL : baseURL ? new URL(baseURL).origin : '',
    basePath: options.basePath || '/api/auth',
    plugins: ...
  }

The 'new URL(baseURL).origin' strips the path back to origin-only. So even though getBaseURL() in utils/url.mjs returns 'https://auth.resu.mx/api/auth' (input + default basePath via withPath()), this line rewrites ctx.baseURL to 'https://auth.resu.mx'.

Then in node_modules/better-auth/dist/api/index.mjs around line 151, the router computes:

  const basePath = new URL(ctx.baseURL).pathname;  // = '/' since ctx.baseURL is origin-only

Routes registered via createAuthEndpoint('/ok', ...) are mounted in better-call's rou3 router at '/ok'. The router's processRequest path computation in node_modules/better-call/dist/router.mjs:

  const path = config?.basePath && config.basePath !== '/' 
    ? pathname.split(config.basePath).reduce(...)
    : url.pathname;

Since config.basePath is '/', the falsy branch: path = url.pathname. For incoming '/api/auth/ok', path = '/api/auth/ok'. No registered route matches this. → 404.

Note: ctx.options.basePath IS set to '/api/auth' (line 88 sets options.basePath = options.basePath || '/api/auth') but the router doesn't consult ctx.options.basePath — it uses ctx.baseURL only.

## What we tried (none fixed it)

1. Set 'basePath: "/api/auth"' explicitly in betterAuth() config — does set ctx.options.basePath but router ignores it; still 404.
2. Added 'trustedOrigins: ["https://auth.resu.mx", "https://resu.mx"]' — no effect (origin check skips GET anyway).
3. Added 'compatibility_flags: ["nodejs_compat"]' to wrangler.jsonc — was already in place but reaffirmed; no effect on routing.
4. URL-strip workaround in our fetch handler: rewrite incoming '/api/auth/ok' to '/ok' before passing to auth.handler(rewritten). Confirmed via debug logs that BetterAuth sees a request to '/ok'. Still 404. (Suspect router's pathname matching has another quirk we haven't traced.)
5. Bypassed Hono and used a plain ExportedHandler (no router middleware between request and BetterAuth). No change.

## Suggested next steps (pick one)

A. Pin to an older BetterAuth (1.4.x or 1.5.x) and retest. The init transformation may have been added recently. cf. release notes.
B. Patch the local BetterAuth via patch-package: change line 88 of context/create-context.mjs to keep the path, OR change line 151 of api/index.mjs to use ctx.options.basePath.
C. File issue upstream at https://github.com/better-auth/better-auth — the router mounting bug looks reproducible in any minimal CF Workers setup.
D. Trace deeper into better-call's createRouter() to confirm the strip-workaround failure mode (debugging revealed BetterAuth saw the rewritten URL but still returned 404 — there's a second mystery).
E. Drop BetterAuth, hand-roll the OAuth dance (sized at 4-6 hrs in earlier discussion).

## Files to look at

- workers/auth/src/auth.ts — current BetterAuth config
- workers/auth/src/index.ts — Worker entry, includes a header comment pointing here
- node_modules/@better-auth/core/dist/context/create-context.mjs — init transformation (line 88)
- node_modules/better-auth/dist/api/index.mjs — router instantiation (line 151)
- node_modules/better-auth/dist/utils/url.mjs — getBaseURL/withPath helpers
- node_modules/better-call/dist/router.mjs — actual route matching

## Spec / plan

Spec at .claude/plans/forge-auth-poc/refs/specs/2026-04-28-forge-auth-poc-design.md.
Plan at .claude/plans/forge-auth-poc/phase/1-poc.md.
PoC bead forge-xk0z.11 closed as 'partially complete' — infrastructure proof done; this bead tracks the remaining OAuth-flow integration.
close_reason: Root cause: explicit baseURL in betterAuth() config makes ctx.options.baseURL truthy, which causes auth/base.mjs:18 (if (!ctx.options.baseURL)) to skip the per-request derivation that would populate ctx.baseURL with the basePath via withPath(). Then api/index.mjs:151 computes basePath = new URL(ctx.baseURL).pathname = '/' and the better-call router (router.mjs:31) cannot strip /api/auth from incoming pathnames, returning 404 for every registered route. Fix: removed 'baseURL: "https://auth.resu.mx"' from src/auth.ts; BetterAuth now derives baseURL from request.url per-request, so basePath resolves correctly to /api/auth and routes match. Verified live: GET /api/auth/ok→200 {ok:true}, POST /api/auth/sign-in/social→200 with GitHub OAuth redirect URL + state cookie (Domain=.resu.mx, Secure, HttpOnly), GET /api/auth/get-session→200 null, GET /api/me→401 (preserved). Worker version 54b736ae deployed at auth.resu.mx. forge-xk0z.13 (auth flow demo page) is now unblocked.

title: Forge Auth Worker PoC — BetterAuth-on-Worker, GitHub OAuth, D1+KV
description: Implement the auth PoC per .claude/plans/forge-auth-poc/refs/specs/2026-04-28-forge-auth-poc-design.md. Stand up forge-auth Worker at auth.resu.mx with BetterAuth + GitHub IdP + D1 user store + KV binding. Out of scope: Google IdP (forge-xk0z.4), JWT migration (forge-xk0z.5), CSRF (forge-xk0z.7), rate limiting (forge-xk0z.8), and 6 other follow-on beads already filed.
close_reason: Partial completion — infrastructure surface proven (Worker deployed, custom domain attached, D1+KV bound, secrets bound, BetterAuth API methods work via /api/me). OAuth round-trip blocked on BetterAuth 1.6.9 HTTP handler integration bug — full diagnostic in forge-xk0z.12. Spec revision history captures the reality.

title: Plan tiers + billing — wire Stripe to Forge SaaS auth
description: Once auth is real, add a billing layer: plan_tier column on users table, Stripe customer + subscription objects, /billing/* endpoints (checkout, portal, webhooks), entitlement checks on paid features. Spans Worker (Stripe webhooks, entitlement reads) + product (UI for billing portal, paywall surfaces). Epic-sized; subtasks created when work starts.

title: Auth: research — BetterAuth vs Svelte Auth Kit vs Dioxus auth libs vs custom for the client side
description: Client-side auth library decision (lives in the forge product repo, not infra, but tracked here for context). The Worker is the server side — what does the browser/mobile client use to manage tokens, refresh, login UX? Options: BetterAuth (full-stack TS, has a CF-Workers adapter), Svelte's auth.js (legacy), roll-your-own with the Worker as backend. Dioxus eventually for native. Decision affects the Worker's API contract.

title: Auth: research — Auth0 / Okta / Clerk / Supabase / Keycloak as managed alternative to roll-our-own
description: Decide whether to keep building the Worker-hosted auth (current path) or migrate to a managed auth platform. Compare on: pricing curve at expected DAU, lock-in cost, custom-flow flexibility (we want OAuth + magic links + MFA + passkeys + B2B SAML eventually), data residency. Existing scope already mentioned in forge-j9by epic.

title: Auth: passwordless signin (magic links) + MFA support
description: Add a non-OAuth signin path for users without GitHub/Google. Magic-link via email (token in KV with short TTL, click-to-confirm). MFA via TOTP (RFC 6238) or WebAuthn/Passkey. Requires email-sending integration (likely Resend or CF Email Workers) and possibly a passkey UX library. Sequenced after hybrid JWT + refresh.

title: Auth: anonymous local → authenticated migration on signup
description: When an anonymous browser user signs up, upload their OPFS-stored Forge state to their newly-created tenant on the server. Touches forge-wasm (export local state), the auth Worker (accept upload at /auth/initial-sync), and forge-server (ingest the bundle). Bigger than a Worker-only change — coordinate with forge product team. Bead is the Worker-side endpoint plus protocol spec.

title: Auth: rate limiting on /auth/* endpoints (CF WAF rules)
description: Prevent brute-force/abuse on the auth endpoints. Use Cloudflare WAF or Workers-native rate limiting: e.g., 10 req/min/IP on /auth/login, 5 req/min/IP on /auth/callback (state validation makes replay hard but rate-limit anyway). Configure via wrangler.jsonc or CF dashboard.

title: Auth: CSRF protection on state-changing /auth/* endpoints
description: Required before any non-PoC use. Implement double-submit cookie or signed-token CSRF protection on /auth/logout and other state-changing routes. /auth/login and /auth/callback already have OAuth state parameter for CSRF defense; this covers the rest.

title: Auth: onboarding routing + new-user-detection in callback
description: Worker-side: when /auth/callback INSERTs a new user row (vs UPDATE), redirect to /onboarding instead of /app. Onboarding UI itself is product-side (see forge-pyy4 children). Captures display name, workspace name, plan tier selection, opt-in to emails. Bead covers the routing logic + a new_user query param hand-off, not the UI.

title: Auth: migrate from opaque-KV sessions to hybrid JWT + refresh-token-in-KV
description: Pattern change driven by per-request KV cost at scale and instant-revocation needs. Issue short-lived (5min) signed JWT for access + opaque refresh token in KV. Most API requests verify JWT only (no KV read); refresh hits KV. Adds JWKS endpoint, key rotation policy, and JWT denylist for emergency revocation.

title: Auth: add Google as IdP #2 to the auth Worker
description: Follow-on to forge-xk0z.1 (GitHub OAuth PoC). Implement the IdentityProvider interface for Google OIDC. Register a Google Cloud OAuth client, add scopes for email + profile, wire callback. Reuses session+user-record code from PoC.

title: root justfile references untracked packages/justfile — fresh checkouts fail every just recipe
description:
## Reproduction

From a fresh clone of main HEAD (or a fresh git worktree branched from it):

```bash
just wasm-build
# error: Could not find source file for module `test`.
#   ——▶ justfile:11:5
#    │
# 11 │ mod test "packages/justfile"
#    │     ^^^^
```

## Current state

- Root `justfile` (committed) declares `mod test "packages/justfile"` near line 11.
- `packages/justfile` exists locally on the maintainer's working tree but is **untracked** (`git status` shows `??`).
- `git ls-files packages/` lists files under `packages/cli/`, `packages/core/`, etc. but no top-level `packages/justfile`.

`just` parses the entire justfile (including module declarations) before executing any recipe, so this single missing file breaks **every** just recipe — `just wasm-build`, `just wasm-deps-check`, `just wasm-pack-build`, `just dev`, `just docker dev`, all of them. CI probably gets around this by invoking `cargo` and `bun` directly, but local-dev workflows are blocked.

Encountered during forge-nst6 worktree work — had to bypass `just` and invoke `cargo build -p forge-wasm --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` directly with the rustup-stable toolchain.

## Fix options

1. **Commit `packages/justfile`** — preferred if the contents are settled. The module presumably contains test-related recipes that just want isolation from the root file.
2. **Remove the `mod test "packages/justfile"` line** from the root justfile until the file is ready to commit. Recipes that lived under `just test ...` would break, but only those.
3. **Stub `packages/justfile`** with a placeholder so module resolution succeeds.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `just --list` succeeds from a fresh checkout of main.
- `just wasm-build` and `just wasm-deps-check` succeed from a fresh checkout (assuming the rustup toolchain is installed per `docs/src/architecture/models/runtime.md`).
- Whoever has been editing `packages/justfile` locally is consulted before committing or reverting.

## Why P2 not P1

Local-dev workflows are broken in fresh worktrees but the maintainer's environment is fine. Workarounds exist (invoke cargo/bun directly), but they undermine the just-recipe documentation. Tied to the same family of "untracked files referenced by committed manifests" issues as the forge-sdk template.rs and forge-server lib.rs beads filed alongside this one.

## Discovered in

forge-nst6 (wa-sqlite OPFS binding), session 2026-04-28.

title: forge-server: Cargo.toml declares missing src/lib.rs — fresh checkouts fail to compile
description:
## Reproduction

From a fresh clone of main HEAD (or a fresh git worktree branched from it):

```bash
cargo check -p forge-server
# error: can't find lib `forge_server` at path `crates/forge-server/src/lib.rs`
```

## Current state

`crates/forge-server/Cargo.toml` declares:

```toml
[lib]
name = "forge_server"
path = "src/lib.rs"
```

But `crates/forge-server/src/lib.rs` does not exist on main HEAD. `git ls-files crates/forge-server/src/` lists `error.rs`, `main.rs`, and the `routes/` tree — no `lib.rs`. The maintainer has `lib.rs`, `db.rs`, `response.rs`, `state.rs`, `routes/templates.rs`, and a `tests/` directory locally as **untracked** files (`git status` shows `??`).

This means anyone cloning the repo cannot compile `forge-server`. Encountered during forge-nst6 worktree work — `cargo check --workspace` failed at this point.

## Fix options

1. **Commit the untracked files** (`lib.rs` + supporting modules + tests/) — preferred if the refactor is settled.
2. **Revert the `[lib]` section in Cargo.toml** until the lib split is ready to land. forge-server runs as `[[bin]]` only; the lib was added in anticipation of integration tests.
3. **Stub `lib.rs`** with a minimal placeholder (`pub fn placeholder() {}`) so the package compiles even if the real lib content isn't ready.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `cargo check -p forge-server` succeeds from a fresh checkout of main.
- `cargo check --workspace` succeeds from a fresh checkout (in conjunction with the parallel forge-sdk template.rs fix).
- Whoever has been editing the untracked files locally is consulted before committing or reverting.

## Why P2 not P1

Same reasoning as the parallel forge-sdk bead — workspace builds are broken for fresh checkouts but maintainer's local environment compiles fine. Dormant defect that becomes visible when anyone branches from main into a clean worktree.

## Discovered in

forge-nst6 (wa-sqlite OPFS binding), session 2026-04-28.
close_reason: Resolved by Cargo.toml [lib] comment-out workaround on forge-lu5s branch (commit 8745049). Re-enable when crates/forge-server/src/lib.rs is committed to main.

title: forge-sdk: stores/mod.rs references untracked template.rs — fresh checkouts fail to compile
description:
## Reproduction

From a fresh clone of main HEAD (or a fresh git worktree branched from it):

```bash
cargo check -p forge-sdk
# error[E0583]: file not found for module `template`
#   --> crates/forge-sdk/src/db/stores/mod.rs:25:1
#    |
# 25 | pub mod template;
#    | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```

## Current state

- `crates/forge-sdk/src/db/stores/mod.rs` declares `pub mod template;` (committed).
- `crates/forge-sdk/src/db/stores/template.rs` exists locally on the maintainer's working tree but is **untracked** (`git status` shows `??`).
- The module declaration is on main HEAD; the file is not. `git ls-files` confirms.

This means anyone cloning the repo, or anyone working in a fresh git worktree branched from main, cannot compile `forge-sdk`. Encountered during forge-nst6 worktree work — `cargo check --workspace` failed at this point.

## Fix options

1. **Commit `template.rs`** — preferred if the implementation is far enough along to be useful.
2. **Comment out / remove `pub mod template;` in mod.rs** until the file is ready to commit.
3. **Stub `template.rs`** with `// in progress, see <bead>` so the workspace at least builds.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `cargo check -p forge-sdk` succeeds from a fresh checkout of main.
- `cargo check --workspace` succeeds from a fresh checkout (in conjunction with the parallel forge-server lib.rs fix).
- Whoever has been editing template.rs locally is consulted before committing or stubbing.

## Why P2 not P1

Workspace builds are broken for fresh checkouts but maintainer's local environment is unaffected. CI may not catch this if CI uses a stale cache or skips forge-sdk specifically. Discovered manually — dormant defect.

## Discovered in

forge-nst6 (wa-sqlite OPFS binding), session 2026-04-28. Also blocks `cargo check --workspace` validation for any future feature-flag changes to forge-core.
close_reason: Resolved by mod-comment workaround on forge-lu5s branch (commit fc13e74). Re-enable when crates/forge-sdk/src/db/stores/template.rs is committed to main.

title: forge-wasm: migrate browser persistence from IDBBatchAtomicVFS to OPFS
description:
Swap the wa-sqlite VFS backing forge-wasm's `Database` from `IDBBatchAtomicVFS` (IndexedDB) to a main-thread async OPFS VFS once one is available without the constraints we faced in forge-nst6.

## Context — why we shipped IDB in forge-nst6

forge-nst6 had two user-approved decisions that turned out to be jointly unsatisfiable against wa-sqlite@1.0.0 (the only published npm version):

- **Decision A** — distribution channel = npm-resolved bundler import (`@rhashimoto/wa-sqlite@1.0.0`).
- **Decision B** — VFS = main-thread async OPFS, defer Web Worker scaffold (deferred to forge-73hi).

wa-sqlite@1.0.0 ships `OriginPrivateFileSystemVFS` (uses `createSyncAccessHandle()` → Worker-only on Chromium/WebKit) and `IDBBatchAtomicVFS` (IndexedDB). The truly main-thread async OPFS VFS (`OPFSAnyContextVFS`) only exists on master HEAD (1.1.1), which is not on npm.

Rather than abandon decision A or B, the lower-risk path was IDB-backed persistence:

- IDBBatchAtomicVFS runs on main thread, async, no Worker scaffold required.
- Browser persistence is functionally equivalent (survives reload, StorageManager quota, clearable via "clear site data").
- The wasm-bindgen surface above the binding is identical regardless of VFS — Svelte/Tauri/Dioxus consumers don't see the difference.
- Migration cost between IDB-backed and OPFS-backed wa-sqlite is bounded: SQL-level export/import (`VACUUM INTO 'export.db'` or dump+restore), no schema or API changes.

## What this bead does

Swap `IDBBatchAtomicVFS` for an OPFS-backed VFS once a viable main-thread async one ships. The work itself is a small Rust+JS edit; the gating question is *which VFS* and *from where*.

## Triggers to revisit

- wa-sqlite publishes 1.1.1 (or later) on npm with `OPFSAnyContextVFS` available — preferred path, keeps decision A intact.
- Project decides supply-chain risk justifies a git-pin to a specific master commit (changes A).
- forge-73hi (Worker upgrade) lands first, in which case the Worker-only `OriginPrivateFileSystemVFS` from npm@1.0.0 becomes viable without this bead.
- Production telemetry from forge-4a01 reveals IDB write throughput is a UI-blocking factor.

## Deliverables

- Replace `IDBBatchAtomicVFS` extern block in `crates/forge-wasm/src/wa_sqlite.rs` with the OPFS VFS bindings (constructor signature differs — `OriginPrivateFileSystemVFS` is `new`-style, `OPFSAnyContextVFS` is async `static create`).
- Update `Database::open` in `crates/forge-wasm/src/database.rs` to construct the new VFS and rename `VFS_NAME`.
- Update `crates/forge-wasm/examples/browser-smoke/` harness to reflect the swap (filenames, copy, expected output).
- One-time data migration: existing users with IDB-backed forge data need a migration path. Either:
  (a) export/import via `VACUUM INTO 'opfs:forge.db'` on first run after upgrade, OR
  (b) run both VFSes for a transition window, OR
  (c) document a manual export-import for early adopters.
  Pick based on user count and tolerance at trigger time.
- `runtime.md` updated with the swap rationale and the migration story.

## Pros/Cons of swap target

| Target | Pros | Cons |
|---|---|---|
| `OPFSAnyContextVFS` from wa-sqlite npm 1.1.1 (when published) | Keeps decision A intact, main-thread async, file-level data shipping option, strategic OPFS target reached | Awaits upstream npm release — uncertain timeline |
| `OPFSAnyContextVFS` via git pin (e.g., master commit) | Available immediately, same OPFS benefits | Changes decision A; git URL deps add supply-chain audit surface (forge-zf8i territory) |
| `OriginPrivateFileSystemVFS` from npm@1.0.0 + Worker (after forge-73hi) | Stays on npm-stable, real OPFS now | Requires forge-73hi to land first |

## Acceptance Criteria

- `Database::open` constructs OPFS-backed VFS, registers it as default, and `open_v2` succeeds against it.
- Browser persistence verified across reloads (the harness round-trip already covers this — same harness, swapped VFS).
- forge-zf8i decision recorded if the swap happens via git pin.
- Existing IDB-backed user data either auto-migrates or has a documented manual migration path.
- runtime.md updated.
- `just wasm-deps-check` still passes (no rusqlite leak).

## Dependencies

- BLOCKED BY (one of): wa-sqlite 1.1.1 on npm, OR forge-73hi (Worker upgrade), OR explicit decision to git-pin (forge-zf8i revisit).

title: forge-wasm: headless-Chrome wasm-pack test harness
description:
Successor to forge-f0gc. Stand up `wasm-pack test --chrome --headless` so forge-wasm has end-to-end tests that exercise the JS↔WASM boundary in a real browser, not just the native rlib path.

## Context

forge-f0gc landed forge-wasm with native cargo tests covering the rlib side of the crate. The cdylib side (wasm-bindgen exports observed from JS) is not yet exercised by automated tests. The wasm-bindgen-test crate is already in dev-dependencies; what is missing is the test runner setup, test files under `tests/`, and CI wiring.

## Deliverables

- tests/web.rs (or similar) with `#[wasm_bindgen_test]` exports that exercise:
  - bundle_version() returns expected string
  - ForgeRuntime::new() + init() + ready getter
  - add() FFI smoke
- wasm-pack test pipeline documented (Chrome, Firefox optional)
- justfile recipe `wasm-test` invoking the harness
- README note in crates/forge-wasm/ on running tests locally

## Acceptance Criteria

- `wasm-pack test --chrome --headless --node` runs and passes
- All exports defined in lib.rs have at least one test exercising the JS-visible API
- justfile recipe produces actionable output
- Optional CI wiring documented (not necessarily implemented in this task)

## Dependencies

- DEPENDS ON: forge-f0gc (crate scaffold) — DONE
- INDEPENDENT of forge-nst6 (wa-sqlite binding) — can run in parallel

## Worktree

.claude/worktrees/forge-wasm/ (shared with all forge-6z5l children)
notes:
Worktree: .claude/worktrees/forge-wasm/ (shared with all forge-6z5l children — they share Cargo.toml workspace edits and crate scaffolding).

## Context handoff from forge-nst6 (2026-04-28)

forge-nst6 (wa-sqlite OPFS binding) ships a hand-runnable browser harness at crates/forge-wasm/examples/opfs_smoke.html (+ accompanying JS) that exercises the full round-trip: open OPFS DB → CREATE TABLE → INSERT → SELECT → assert. It is the manual analog of what 901c will automate.

When picking up 901c, the work is:
1. Convert the round-trip exercised in opfs_smoke.html into tests/web.rs (or split files) using #[wasm_bindgen_test].
2. Add OPFS round-trip coverage on top of the bundle_version/ForgeRuntime/add() FFI smoke specified in this bead's original deliverables.
3. The wa-sqlite binding is async — tests will need wasm_bindgen_test async support.
4. OPFS persistence-across-reload cannot be exercised in a single wasm-pack test run; document the boundary and either skip that branch or stand up a multi-page Playwright harness as a follow-up.

The async OPFS VFS chosen in forge-nst6 works on main thread, so headless-Chrome wasm-pack test (which runs in a main-thread context) is sufficient to exercise the binding. If/when forge-73hi (AccessHandlePool / Worker upgrade) lands, the harness will need a Worker entry point.

Why this bead can run independently of forge-nst6: original deliverables (rlib-side smoke tests) don't require the wa-sqlite binding. After forge-nst6 closes, the harness should be extended to cover the binding — not a blocker, just an expansion of test surface.

title: Market Intelligence Pipeline
description:
Server-side JD scraping, federated user contributions, deduplication, and aggregation into global market stats. Published as part of the global skill graph snapshot.

## Architecture

Two data sources:
1. Server-side baseline: JobSpy scraping, public job board feeds → extraction → aggregation
2. Federated user contributions: browser extraction produces anonymized tuples → POST to server on sync → dedup → aggregate

Dedup key: hash(normalize(org_name) + normalize(role_title) + posting_quarter)

Output: skill_demand_stats rows aggregated per time_window, published in global snapshot.

See docs/src/architecture/graphs/computed.md for data model and query patterns.

## Dependencies

- DEPENDS ON: forge-czgq (Extraction Pipeline) — extraction produces the skill tuples
- DEPENDS ON: forge-ucc2 (Skill Graph Data Model) — market stats are part of the graph snapshot

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Server-side JD scraping produces skill extraction tuples
- [ ] Federated contribution endpoint accepts anonymized tuples
- [ ] Dedup prevents double-counting same JD from multiple users
- [ ] skill_demand_stats aggregation produces per-window statistics
- [ ] Market stats included in global snapshot
- [ ] Temporal trend queries work (rising/declining skill demand)
- [ ] Industry and archetype breakdowns when volume supports it
notes: DOCUMENTATION REQUIREMENT: Update docs/src/architecture/graphs/computed.md with actual market stats pipeline implementation, dedup key format, and aggregation methodology.

title: CRDT Sync Protocol
description:
Design and implement the CRDT-based sync protocol for browser wa-sqlite to D1 synchronization. Enables multi-device SaaS usage.

## Architecture

Browser wa-sqlite maintains a mutation log of CRDT operations. On sync: push ops to server, server merges into D1, server returns remote ops, browser applies.

## CRDT Types Per Entity

- LWW Register per field: sources, bullets, JDs, orgs, perspectives, summaries, notes, alignment results
- LSeq / Fractional Index: resume section ordering (DnD conflicts)
- Add-Wins OR-Set: resume entries (membership), skill tags (junction tables)
- Append-only: career target snapshots (immutable once created)

## Session Lifecycle

1. Session start: pull ops from D1 since last sync via Turso diff
2. During session: all mutations local (wa-sqlite), logged as CRDT ops
3. Session end / periodic: push ops, receive remote ops, merge
4. Between sessions: D1 is source of truth

## Zero-Knowledge Compatibility

Design encryption seam now (passthrough impl). CRDT merge works on metadata (timestamps, actor IDs) without decrypting payloads. Market stat contributions are NOT encrypted.

See docs/src/architecture/models/sync.md for full protocol design.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] CRDT operation log captures all wa-sqlite mutations
- [ ] LWW merge resolves field-level conflicts correctly
- [ ] OR-Set merge preserves concurrent additions
- [ ] LSeq merge handles concurrent reordering without data loss
- [ ] Sync roundtrip: browser A → D1 → browser B works correctly
- [ ] Offline accumulation: 100+ pending ops sync correctly on reconnect
- [ ] Encryption seam interface defined (passthrough implementation)
- [ ] Version vector tracking per device
notes: DOCUMENTATION REQUIREMENT: Update docs/src/architecture/models/sync.md with actual CRDT implementation details, merge semantics, and conflict resolution test results.
